channel.title: unmask.tools — Institutional Double-Speak Wire
channel.description: Autonomous linguistic audit tracking technocratic evasion and structural violence codes. Every item is a phrase verified by human reviewers to abstract physical harm.

item.title: FLAGGED LANGUAGE: "stranded assets"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “stranded assets”

Physical reality: Fossil fuel reserves or infrastructure that cannot be monetised due to climate policy, described from investor perspective rather than perspective of communities protected.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-1 — Climate Change

Severity: FLAGGED LANGUAGE · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: FLAGGED LANGUAGE: "soil carbon sequestration"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “soil carbon sequestration”

Physical reality: Storage of carbon in agricultural soil through land management, which may be used as a climate offset while concealing the complex conditions required for carbon stability.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-1 — Climate Change

Severity: FLAGGED LANGUAGE · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: FLAGGED LANGUAGE: "irrigation efficiency"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “irrigation efficiency”

Physical reality: Water management framing that abstracts both the depletion of shared water sources and the redistribution of water access between upstream and downstream communities.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-4 — Freshwater Use

Severity: FLAGGED LANGUAGE · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: FLAGGED LANGUAGE: "environmental impact assessment"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “environmental impact assessment”

Physical reality: A procedural document that, once completed, permits physical environmental harm up to the assessed level, converting impact evaluation into approval mechanism.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-9 — Novel Entities and Chemical Pollution

Severity: FLAGGED LANGUAGE · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: FLAGGED LANGUAGE: "decarbonisation pathway"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “decarbonisation pathway”

Physical reality: A planned sequence of emission reductions that abstracts physical harm occurring to specific populations during years the pathway remains incomplete.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-1 — Climate Change

Severity: FLAGGED LANGUAGE · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: FLAGGED LANGUAGE: "climate transition"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “climate transition”

Physical reality: Language suggesting an orderly energy shift that abstracts physical consequences of flooding, drought, and heat displacement experienced during the transition.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-1 — Climate Change

Severity: FLAGGED LANGUAGE · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "water stress"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “water stress”

Physical reality: People lacking access to sufficient safe drinking water or irrigation water for food production, described as a technical metric rather than a crisis of access.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-4 — Freshwater Use

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "waste stream"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “waste stream”

Physical reality: The flow of toxic, hazardous, or non-degradable material through industrial processes into the environment, described in logistics language rather than as contamination.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-9 — Novel Entities and Chemical Pollution

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "sustainable yield"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “sustainable yield”

Physical reality: Extraction rates modeled as sustainable through stock management approaches that may underestimate ecosystem complexity and cumulative impacts.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-2 — Biosphere Integrity

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "sustainable harvesting"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “sustainable harvesting”

Physical reality: Extraction rates described as sustainable based on stock management models that may understate ecological complexity, genetic diversity loss, and non-target impacts.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-2 — Biosphere Integrity

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "safe levels"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “safe levels”

Physical reality: Regulatory thresholds for chemical or radiation exposure set through political and economic processes that may not reflect the full body of scientific evidence on health effects, particularly for vulnerable populations.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-9 — Novel Entities and Chemical Pollution

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "responsible disposal"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “responsible disposal”

Physical reality: The transfer of toxic materials to a location where their physical harm is more distant from production sites, concealing harm redistribution.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-9 — Novel Entities and Chemical Pollution

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "residual risk"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “residual risk”

Physical reality: The portion of physical harm from industrial activity that is accepted as an outcome of regulatory approval, normalising harm as inevitable.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-9 — Novel Entities and Chemical Pollution

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "radiative forcing"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “radiative forcing”

Physical reality: The net energy imbalance in Earth's atmosphere caused by accumulated greenhouse gases, trapping heat and driving temperature increases that destabilize climate patterns.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-1 — Climate Change

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "permissible exposure limits"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “permissible exposure limits”

Physical reality: Regulatory ceilings for chemical or radiation exposure that are politically negotiated thresholds rather than scientific measures of safety, particularly inadequate for vulnerable populations.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-9 — Novel Entities and Chemical Pollution

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "legacy contamination"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “legacy contamination”

Physical reality: Historical pollution whose physical effects persist in soil, water, and human bodies, described in past tense to imply harm has ended when exposure continues.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-9 — Novel Entities and Chemical Pollution

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "green growth"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “green growth”

Physical reality: The proposition that economic expansion can decouple from environmental harm, abstracting evidence that absolute decoupling from material throughput has not been demonstrated.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-1 — Climate Change

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "extreme heat events"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “extreme heat events”

Physical reality: Increased frequency and intensity of dangerously high temperatures that cause heat stroke, crop failure, water depletion, and mass mortality among vulnerable populations, particularly the elderly and outdoor workers.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-1 — Climate Change

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "emissions trading scheme"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “emissions trading scheme”

Physical reality: A market mechanism for redistributing the right to pollute that does not reduce physical harm from the emissions traded.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-1 — Climate Change

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "carbon offsets"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “carbon offsets”

Physical reality: Financial instruments allowing entities to emit greenhouse gases by funding offsetting activities elsewhere, which do not reduce physical atmospheric pollution.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-1 — Climate Change

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "carbon leakage"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “carbon leakage”

Physical reality: Relocation of carbon-intensive industrial production to jurisdictions with weaker regulation, described as economic phenomenon rather than transfer of ecosystem harm.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-1 — Climate Change

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "biodiversity offsetting"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “biodiversity offsetting”

Physical reality: Financial mechanisms permitting destruction of one ecosystem provided equivalent ecosystem is created elsewhere, although destroyed species and relationships are not fungible.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-2 — Biosphere Integrity

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "biodiversity net gain"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “biodiversity net gain”

Physical reality: Accounting mechanisms permitting ecosystem destruction provided equivalent ecosystem area is created or protected elsewhere, abstracting non-fungible species loss.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-2 — Biosphere Integrity

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "agricultural intensification"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “agricultural intensification”

Physical reality: Increasing crop yields through chemical inputs, mechanisation, and genetic modification while concealing associated soil degradation, aquifer depletion, and chemical pollution.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-3, PB-4, PB-5

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "acceptable limits"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “acceptable limits”

Physical reality: Regulatory exposure thresholds established through political negotiation rather than scientific consensus, permitting health harm deemed politically acceptable rather than scientifically safe.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-9 — Novel Entities and Chemical Pollution

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: CRITICAL VIOLATION: "transgressing planetary boundaries"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “transgressing planetary boundaries”

Physical reality: Exceeding the biophysical thresholds within which Earth systems remain stable, causing irreversible damage and triggering tipping points that destabilize global conditions.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries Framework — Foundational Principles

Severity: CRITICAL VIOLATION · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: CRITICAL VIOLATION: "tipping points"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “tipping points”

Physical reality: Critical thresholds in Earth systems beyond which irreversible shifts occur and ecosystems transition to qualitatively different, often degraded states that support fewer people and less biodiversity.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries Framework — Core Principle: Physical Irreversibility

Severity: CRITICAL VIOLATION · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: CRITICAL VIOLATION: "sea level rise displacement"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “sea level rise displacement”

Physical reality: The forced evacuation of hundreds of millions of people from coastal cities and agricultural zones due to rising seawater inundating land, destroying homes, infrastructure, and food production capacity.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-1 — Climate Change

Severity: CRITICAL VIOLATION · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: CRITICAL VIOLATION: "safe operating space"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “safe operating space”

Physical reality: A defined biophysical threshold beyond which Earth system changes become irreversible or cause severe harm to human populations and ecosystems.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries Framework — Rockström et al. (2009); Richardson et al. (2023)

Severity: CRITICAL VIOLATION · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: CRITICAL VIOLATION: "permafrost thaw feedback"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “permafrost thaw feedback”

Physical reality: The melting of permanently frozen soil releasing methane and CO2 stored for millennia, accelerating atmospheric warming and triggering further permafrost thaw in a self-reinforcing cycle that humans cannot stop even with emission reductions.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries Framework — Core Principle: Physical Irreversibility

Severity: CRITICAL VIOLATION · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: CRITICAL VIOLATION: "non-linear abrupt changes"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “non-linear abrupt changes”

Physical reality: Sudden, unpredictable shifts in Earth system behavior that cross tipping points and trigger irreversible cascading ecosystem collapse.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries Framework — Foundational Principles

Severity: CRITICAL VIOLATION · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: CRITICAL VIOLATION: "monsoon system disruption"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “monsoon system disruption”

Physical reality: The collapse or severe alteration of monsoon precipitation patterns due to climate change, eliminating the water supply of over 3 billion people and triggering mass famine and forced displacement across Asia and Africa.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries Framework — Core Principle: Physical Irreversibility

Severity: CRITICAL VIOLATION · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: CRITICAL VIOLATION: "ice sheet collapse"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “ice sheet collapse”

Physical reality: The irreversible melting and disintegration of the West Antarctic and Greenland ice sheets, releasing stored water and raising global sea levels by metres, rendering coastal cities uninhabitable and displacing hundreds of millions of people.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries Framework — Core Principle: Physical Irreversibility

Severity: CRITICAL VIOLATION · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: CRITICAL VIOLATION: "groundwater drawdown"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “groundwater drawdown”

Physical reality: The extraction of groundwater at rates exceeding natural recharge, resulting in permanent loss of freshwater reserves essential for survival.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-4 — Freshwater Use

Severity: CRITICAL VIOLATION · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: CRITICAL VIOLATION: "extinction rate acceleration"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “extinction rate acceleration”

Physical reality: The rate at which species are disappearing from Earth increasing 100 to 1,000 times above natural background rates due to habitat destruction, pollution, and climate change, constituting a mass extinction event.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-2 — Biosphere Integrity (Biodiversity Loss)

Severity: CRITICAL VIOLATION · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: CRITICAL VIOLATION: "coral reef ecosystem collapse"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “coral reef ecosystem collapse”

Physical reality: The death of coral reef systems from bleaching and acidification, eliminating the food source and storm protection of 500 million coastal people and destroying the foundation of marine biodiversity.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries Framework — Core Principle: Physical Irreversibility

Severity: CRITICAL VIOLATION · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: CRITICAL VIOLATION: "coral bleaching reef death"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “coral bleaching reef death”

Physical reality: The expulsion of symbiotic algae from coral tissues in response to warmer ocean temperatures, causing coral death and eliminating the foundation of reef ecosystems and the survival of 500 million dependent people.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-1 — Climate Change

Severity: CRITICAL VIOLATION · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: CRITICAL VIOLATION: "aquifer depletion"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “aquifer depletion”

Physical reality: The permanent loss of freshwater reserves that populations depend on for drinking, sanitation, and food production, described in hydrological terms.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries PB-4 — Freshwater Use

Severity: CRITICAL VIOLATION · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: CRITICAL VIOLATION: "amazon rainforest dieback"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “amazon rainforest dieback”

Physical reality: The collapse of the Amazon ecosystem from a carbon sink and climate-regulating forest to a degraded savanna that releases stored carbon and dramatically reduces regional rainfall, affecting billions of people.

Legal anchor: Planetary Boundaries Framework — Core Principle: Physical Irreversibility

Severity: CRITICAL VIOLATION · Confidence: 90% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "discretionary spending"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “discretionary spending”

Physical reality: Resource Parasitism — reclassification of essential biological survival expenditure (food, water, shelter, health) as optional line items subject to fiscal austerity

Legal anchor: unmask.shield §10.2 — UDHR Art. 25 / UN Charter Art. 55

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 95% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: FLAGGED LANGUAGE: "natural capital"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “natural capital”

Physical reality: Resource Parasitism — monetisation of the biological commons; frames the non-negotiable foundations of physical survival as balance-sheet assets available for drawdown

Legal anchor: unmask.shield §10.2 — Planetary Boundaries Framework / Ecocide Framework

Severity: FLAGGED LANGUAGE · Confidence: 95% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: FLAGGED LANGUAGE: "ecosystem utility"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “ecosystem utility”

Physical reality: Resource Parasitism — reduction of living habitat systems to economic utility functions, erasing their intrinsic biological value and right to exist

Legal anchor: unmask.shield §10.2 — Planetary Boundaries Framework

Severity: FLAGGED LANGUAGE · Confidence: 95% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: FLAGGED LANGUAGE: "carbon credits"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “carbon credits”

Physical reality: Resource Parasitism — financialisation of atmospheric commons, converting a planetary boundary into a tradeable commodity that permits continued emission of thermal stress

Legal anchor: unmask.shield §10.2 — Planetary Boundaries Framework / Ecocide Framework

Severity: FLAGGED LANGUAGE · Confidence: 95% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "sustainable extraction"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “sustainable extraction”

Physical reality: Resource Parasitism — oxymoronic framing that cloaks ongoing depletion of biological commons under an ecological legitimacy claim

Legal anchor: unmask.shield §10.2 — Planetary Boundaries Framework (Hard Ceiling)

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 95% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: FLAGGED LANGUAGE: "resource allocation"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “resource allocation”

Physical reality: Resource Parasitism — reframing of extraction from biological commons (water, land, food security) as a neutral technical process

Legal anchor: unmask.shield §10.2 — Planetary Boundaries Framework / UN Charter Art. 55

Severity: FLAGGED LANGUAGE · Confidence: 95% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "force majeure"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “force majeure”

Physical reality: Malingering for Legal Evasion — reclassification of foreseeable physical harm as unforeseeable 'act of nature' to evade accountability

Legal anchor: unmask.shield §10.2 — Rome Statute / Geneva Conv. Art. 3

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 95% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "liability waiver"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “liability waiver”

Physical reality: Malingering for Legal Evasion — pre-emptive removal of accountability for physical consequences before they are inflicted on a biological body

Legal anchor: unmask.shield §10.2 — Rome Statute Art. 25

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 95% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: FLAGGED LANGUAGE: "health and safety compliance"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “health and safety compliance”

Physical reality: Malingering for Legal Evasion — procedural framing that displaces accountability for physical harm onto regulatory abstractions

Legal anchor: unmask.shield §10.2 — Rome Statute / UDHR Art. 25

Severity: FLAGGED LANGUAGE · Confidence: 95% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "medical exemption"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “medical exemption”

Physical reality: Malingering for Legal Evasion — medical abstractions used to shield a physical body or institution from legal accountability for habitat harm

Legal anchor: unmask.shield §10.2 — Rome Statute Art. 17

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 95% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools

item.title: HIGH CONCERN: "unintended consequences"
item.description:
Institutional phrase: “unintended consequences”

Physical reality: Physical harm to persons or ecosystems classified as unforeseen — used to reduce accountability for foreseeable outcomes

Legal anchor: Rome Statute Art. 25 — Individual Criminal Responsibility

Severity: HIGH CONCERN · Confidence: 95% · Language: EN · Confirmed 0 time(s).

Verified via decentralised human-in-the-loop consensus. unmask.tools
